title: Convert string to number

content.problemStatement:
You are given a string that represents a positive number. Your task is to write a program that converts this string into its numerical equivalent without using any in-built parsing, conversion libraries, or direct type casting methods. The string will not contain any leading zeros, decimals, or any non-numeric characters.

Complete the function **stringToNumber** in the IDE


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer, T, the number of test cases.
The following T lines each contain a single string, S, representing the number.


Output Format
For each test case, print the numerical equivalent of the string.
constraints: S contains only characters '0'-'9' and has no leading zeros
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty string S consisting only of digits (0‑9) without leading zeros.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a line containing the integer value represented by S.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each string is converted digit by digit to its integer value.
Single‑digit and two‑digit numbers are handled identically.
A larger number still fits within 64‑bit and is correctly reconstructed.

solution.approach: We read each string character by character and build the numeric value using the classic positional method: start with result = 0 and for each digit d update result = result * 10 + d. This works because the strings represent positive integers and their length is at most 10, so the result fits in a 64‑bit signed integer. The algorithm is linear in the total number of characters across all test cases.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total number of digits) overall, i.e. O(|S|) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides input storage.

code.Java: import java.io.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws IOException{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));String line=br.readLine();if(line==null)return;int T=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int t=0;t<T;t++){String S=br.readLine().trim();long num=0;for(int i=0;i<S.length();i++){num=num*10+(S.charAt(i)-'0');}sb.append(num);if(t+1<T)sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
